top_issues.title: Clutch and dual-mass flywheel failure on 1.0 EcoBoost and 1.6 diesel engines
top_issues.description: Early clutch wear and failure of the dual-mass flywheel (DMF) is a widespread issue affecting 1.0 EcoBoost and 1.6 diesel variants, often occurring between 5,000 and 50,000 miles. The root cause is frequently a faulty clutch slave cylinder that leaks hydraulic fluid, contaminating the clutch plates and DMF. Repair costs are substantial and warranty coverage is often disputed by Ford.
typical_symptoms: Clutch judder or shudder when pulling away from rest
Burning smell from the engine bay
Difficulty engaging gears, especially 2nd gear when warm
Loss of drive or slipping sensation during acceleration
Grinding or knocking noise from the transmission area

top_issues.title: 1.0 EcoBoost engine coolant system failure and degas pipe cracking
top_issues.description: The plastic degas pipe connecting the turbo to the expansion tank is prone to cracking or splitting, particularly on early 2012 and 2013 models. This causes coolant loss, engine overheating, and in severe cases, complete engine failure with cracked cylinder heads. The original black-union pipe was later replaced with a modified white-union version, but failures have continued even after the recall modification.
typical_symptoms: Coolant loss visible under the engine or in the engine bay
Engine overheating warning light on dashboard
White smoke or steam from under the bonnet
Loss of power and 'service now' warning light
Coolant in the oil or oil in the coolant system

top_issues.title: Powershift automatic transmission judder and clutch pack failure
top_issues.description: The six-speed dry-clutch Powershift automatic transmission is prone to juddering, especially when pulling away from rest or changing from 1st to 2nd gear. The dry clutch pack can fail prematurely, and transmission fluid leaks can contaminate the clutches. Ford has issued multiple software updates and clutch replacements, but problems often recur. Transmission fluid must be changed every three years to prevent excessive wear.
typical_symptoms: Juddering or jerking sensation when accelerating from standstill
Delay in gear changes or hesitation between gears
Grinding or rattling noise from the transmission
Transmission Control Module (TCM) failure warning light
Car entering limp mode or loss of drive

top_issues.title: Clutch slave cylinder leakage on 1.6 diesel engines
top_issues.description: The clutch slave cylinder on 1.6 diesel variants (DV6C engine) is prone to internal leakage, allowing hydraulic fluid to contaminate the clutch plates and dual-mass flywheel. This leads to premature clutch wear, DMF damage, and eventual failure. The problem often goes undiagnosed initially, with symptoms mistaken for driver error or normal wear.
typical_symptoms: Sticking or spongy clutch pedal
Difficulty engaging gears or grinding when selecting gears
Clutch slip during acceleration
Burning smell from the clutch area
Visible hydraulic fluid leakage near the transmission bell housing

top_issues.title: Heater matrix and coolant system leaks
top_issues.description: Heater matrix failures and coolant leaks from various seals and unions are common, particularly on 1.0 EcoBoost models. Leaks can occur from the heater matrix itself, the union between the matrix and engine coolant pipe, or from seals behind the timing cover. Repair often requires extensive engine dismantling and can be costly.
typical_symptoms: Sweet-smelling coolant odour inside the cabin
Wet carpets or puddles in the passenger footwell
Coolant loss without visible external leaks
Fogging of windows
Scalding hot water dripping into the footwell

overall_reliability_note: The 2011–2014 Focus is generally practical and comfortable, but early examples are plagued by clutch and dual-mass flywheel failures, particularly on 1.0 EcoBoost and 1.6 diesel engines, and the Powershift automatic transmission is unreliable with recurring judder and clutch pack problems. Coolant system issues on 1.0 EcoBoost models can lead to catastrophic engine failure.
